extra.message: User-controlled input from request.POST['first_name'] is base64-decoded and passed directly to exec(), allowing arbitrary remote code execution on the server. Both Python 3 (line 72) and Python 2 (line 76) fallback paths are vulnerable.

User-controlled 'p' query parameter is used directly in os.path.join() and open() without sanitization. An attacker can traverse directories using '../' sequences to read arbitrary files on the filesystem (e.g., settings.py, /etc/passwd).

User-controlled 'fwd' query parameter is used to look up a function name from globals() and then call it with the request object. This allows an attacker to invoke any function in the module's global scope (e.g., 'admin', 'unvalidated_redirect'), bypassing intended access controls.

User-controlled 'url' query parameter is passed directly to Django's redirect() without any validation or whitelisting. An attacker can redirect users to arbitrary external websites, enabling phishing attacks.

The missing_access_control view provides access to the admin interface based solely on a query parameter (?action=admin) with no authentication, session check, or authorization. Any unauthenticated user can access the admin page.

The dor_user_profile view uses the userid from the URL to look up and display user profiles with no authentication or authorization check. Any user can view (line 130) and modify (lines 133-134) any other user's profile data including name and email by simply changing the userid in the URL path.

User-controlled 'qs' query parameter is rendered directly into a JavaScript context with the Django |safe filter, bypassing auto-escaping. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript that executes in the victim's browser (reflected XSS). The source is request.GET['qs'] in views.py:110.

User-controlled URL path variable is rendered directly into a JavaScript context with the Django |safe filter, bypassing auto-escaping. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript via the URL path (reflected XSS). The source is the URL capture group in views.py:105.

User-controlled 'qs' query parameter is rendered directly into an HTML input value attribute with the Django |safe filter, bypassing auto-escaping. An attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript that executes in the victim's browser (reflected XSS). The source is request.GET['qs'] in views.py:98.

User-controlled 'qs' query parameter is rendered directly into a JavaScript context with the Django |safe filter, bypassing auto-escaping. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript that executes in the victim's browser (reflected XSS). The source is request.GET['qs'] in views.py:166.

CSRF middleware (CsrfViewMiddleware) is globally commented out in MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES, leaving all POST endpoints vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery attacks. Combined with the fact that no views use CSRF tokens in forms, an attacker can forge requests on behalf of users.
The @csrf_exempt decorator explicitly disables CSRF protection on the csrf_image view, leaving it vulnerable to CSRF attacks even if global CSRF middleware were enabled.

Django SECRET_KEY is hardcoded in source code. This key is used for session signing, CSRF tokens, password reset tokens, and other cryptographic operations. An attacker with this key can forge session cookies and tokens.

DEBUG mode is enabled (DEBUG = True). In production, this will display detailed error pages with stack traces, local variables, and source code context to users, exposing sensitive application internals and configuration.

The boom view raises a generic Exception('boom'). With DEBUG=True in settings.py, Django will render a detailed debug page showing the full stack trace, local variables, source code, and environment configuration, leaking sensitive information about the application.

cwe: CWE-209: Information Exposure Through an Error Message

extra.message: XFrameOptionsMiddleware is commented out in MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES. Without X-Frame-Options headers, the application can be embedded in iframes on attacker-controlled sites, making it vulnerable to clickjacking attacks.

cwe: CWE-1021: Improper Restriction of Rendered UI Layers or Frames

extra.message: The debug_toolbar SHOW_TOOLBAR_CALLBACK allows any user to enable the Django Debug Toolbar by passing a 'debug' parameter in the query string, POST body, or cookie. This exposes SQL queries, settings, request data, and other sensitive application internals.
